---
title: "Homework 1: Simple Linear Regression"
---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Type** | Conceptual |
| **Due** | Session 3 |
| **Weight** | Part of Homework (50% of final grade) |
| **Grading** | Completion-based |
| **Related Reading** | [Chapter 1: Simple Linear Regression]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}/chapters/01-simple-linear-regression.html){target="_blank"} |
| **Related Lab** | [Lab 2: Simple Linear Regression]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}/labs/lab02-simple-regression.html){target="_blank"} |

::: {.callout-note}
## Grading Policy
Homework is graded on **completion**. Submit a good-faith effort for full credit. A good-faith effort means: (1) all problems are attempted, (2) for computational assignments, R code runs without errors, and (3) written interpretations are included where requested.
:::

## Problems

### Problem 1

For each of the following pairs of variables, identify which is the **response variable** ($y$) and which is the **explanatory variable** ($x$). Briefly justify your choice.

a. College GPA and High School GPA
b. Number of children and Mother's education level
c. Annual income and Years of education
d. Annual income and Home value

### Problem 2

For values of $x$ between 0 and 10, sketch each of the following lines on the same graph. Label each line clearly.

a. $y = 7 + 0.5x$
b. $y = 7 + x$
c. $y = 7 - x$
d. $y = 7 - 0.5x$
e. $y = 7$
f. $y = x$

### Problem 3

A college admissions officer claims that the relationship between college GPA ($y$) and high school GPA ($x$) can be described by the regression equation $\hat{y} = 0.5 + 7x$.

a. Is this equation realistic? Explain why or why not.
b. Suppose the actual equation is $\hat{y} = 0.5 + 0.7x$. Interpret the slope in context.
c. Using the equation from (b), predict the college GPA for a student with a high school GPA of 3.0. Repeat for a high school GPA of 4.0.
d. Suppose instead that $\hat{y} = x$. Interpret this equation in context. What would it mean for the relationship between college GPA and high school GPA?


## Submission

1. Complete all problems, showing your work clearly.
2. Submit your completed assignment as a **PDF** to Blackboard by the due date.
